mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold over a five-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases over a ten-year out-of-sample backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during periods of capacity constraints or supply chain bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market absorbs inflation shocks without drawdowns, yielding positive returns in greater than 65 percent of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment fails to predict returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to correlate with subsequent market direction, producing a t-statistic below 1.5 over ten years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market rallies on production declines in greater than 60 percent of cases due to aggressive liquidity injections or fiscal stimulus.
